chapterSummary: Harry awakens in the hospital wing to Dumbledore's presence, learning the truth about Quirrell's possession by Voldemort and Snape's protective actions. Dumbledore reveals the Sorcerer's Stone has been destroyed, ensuring Voldemort's continued weakness, and explains the power of Harry's mother's love. Harry, Ron, and Hermione celebrate Gryffindor's unexpected House Cup victory at the end-of-year feast, and Harry returns to the Dursleys, now armed with the knowledge of his magical identity and a plan to torment Dudley.

knowledge.content: He learns that Professor Quirrell was possessed by Voldemort and was the true antagonist trying to steal the Sorcerer's Stone.
He learns that Snape was actually trying to protect him during the Quidditch match and was not trying to kill him.
He learns that Snape hated his father, James Potter, but was in his debt because James saved Snape's life.
He learns that the Mirror of Erised was enchanted by Dumbledore to give the Stone only to someone who wanted to find it, not use it.
He learns that the Sorcerer's Stone has been destroyed.
He learns that Nicolas Flamel and his wife will die now that the Stone is destroyed, but Dumbledore views death as 'the next great adventure'.
He learns from Dumbledore that 'fear of a name increases fear of the thing itself' and should call Voldemort by his name.
He learns that his mother's love, a powerful magic, left a protective mark on him that Voldemort cannot understand or touch.
He learns that his Invisibility Cloak belonged to his father, James Potter, who used it for sneaking around.
He receives a photo album filled with pictures of his parents and their friends from Hagrid.
He receives sixty points for Gryffindor for 'pure nerve and outstanding courage'.
He observes the entire school knows about his adventure with Quirrell.
He plans to use the rule against magic outside of school to torment Dudley during the summer.

locations.name: Hospital Wing
locations.description: A clean, white-linened infirmary bed with a table piled high with candy.
lore: A place for recovery and healing at Hogwarts.
Madam Pomfrey is the strict but caring nurse in charge.

locations.name: Last Chamber
locations.description: The final chamber after the potion puzzle, where Quirrell and Voldemort await.
lore: The location where the Sorcerer's Stone is hidden and the final confrontation takes place.

locations.name: Great Hall
locations.description: A magnificent hall decked out in Slytherin colors, later magically transformed to Gryffindor colors for the feast.
lore: The setting for the end-of-year feast and the awarding of the House Cup.
The ceiling is bewitched to reflect the sky outside.

locations.name: Hogwarts Express
locations.description: A scarlet steam engine transporting students back to London, with a festive atmosphere.
lore: The traditional mode of transport for Hogwarts students to and from school.
A place for students to socialize and reflect on the school year.

locations.name: Platform Nine and Three-Quarters
locations.description: A bustling, magical platform at King's Cross, now filled with departing students.
lore: The hidden gateway between the Muggle and wizarding worlds.

locations.name: King's Cross Station
locations.description: A busy Muggle train station, the final destination for the Hogwarts Express.
lore: A point of transition between the magical and Muggle worlds.

locations.name: Gateway to Muggle World
locations.description: The barrier between Platform Nine and Three-Quarters and the Muggle side of King's Cross.
lore: The final threshold separating Harry's two worlds.

threads.description: The Dursleys' desperate attempt to maintain normalcy and hide their connection to the magical world.
threads.development: The Dursleys are terrified of Harry, reinforcing their desire to avoid magic, but Harry now plans to use his knowledge of their fear against them.

threads.description: The mystery surrounding the strange events (owls, shooting stars, cloaked people) and their connection to the magical world.
threads.development: The mystery of the strange events is fully explained as the result of Voldemort's defeat and the subsequent magical celebrations.

threads.description: The fate of the Potters and their son, Harry, after Voldemort's attack.
threads.development: The immediate threat to Harry from Voldemort is resolved with the destruction of the Stone, and Harry learns the truth about his parents' sacrifice and the protective power of his mother's love.

threads.description: Harry's abusive upbringing and isolation by the Dursleys.
threads.development: Harry's physical separation from the Dursleys for the summer reinforces the resolution of this thread, though his plan to torment Dudley hints at future interactions.

threads.description: Harry's burgeoning magical abilities and his unconscious connection to the magical world.
threads.development: Harry's magical abilities are confirmed and understood, and his connection to the magical world is fully embraced, culminating in his successful use of magic against Quirrell.

threads.description: Harry's journey to Hogwarts and his introduction to the wizarding world.
threads.development: This thread is fully resolved as Harry completes his first year at Hogwarts, having been fully integrated into the wizarding world.

threads.description: The mystery of the item in Gringotts vault 713.
threads.development: The item is revealed to be the Sorcerer's Stone, which is subsequently destroyed, resolving the mystery.

threads.description: The developing animosity between Harry Potter and Professor Snape.
threads.development: Harry learns Snape was protecting him, not harming him, but also learns of Snape's deep-seated hatred for his father, complicating their relationship but shifting the animosity from life-threatening to personal.

threads.description: The rivalry between Gryffindor and Slytherin houses.
threads.development: Gryffindor's dramatic victory in the House Cup temporarily resolves the immediate tension, but the underlying rivalry remains.

threads.description: The burgeoning friendship between Harry, Ron, and Hermione.
threads.development: Their shared ordeal and mutual support solidify their friendship, confirming its strength and permanence.

threads.description: The mystery of who is trying to steal the item guarded by the three-headed dog.
threads.development: Quirrell is revealed as the thief, possessed by Voldemort, and the Stone is destroyed, resolving the mystery.

threads.description: Harry's search for his family and understanding his past.
threads.development: Harry learns the full truth about his parents' sacrifice, the protective power of his mother's love, and receives a photo album, bringing closure to this search for now.

threads.description: The mystery and dangers of the Mirror of Erised.
threads.development: The Mirror's purpose is fully revealed, and its role in protecting the Stone is explained, resolving its mystery.

threads.description: Hagrid's dangerous secret: an illegal dragon.
threads.development: Norbert has been successfully rehomed, and Hagrid's guilt over revealing Fluffy's secret is addressed, bringing this thread to a close.

scenes.summary: Harry confronts Quirrell, who reveals himself as Voldemort's host and the true antagonist. Quirrell confesses to jinxing Harry's broom and letting in the troll, while Snape was secretly protecting Harry. Voldemort, a terrifying face on the back of Quirrell's head, commands Quirrell to force Harry to reveal the Stone's location in the Mirror of Erised. Harry, realizing the Mirror's function, sees the Stone in his reflection and it drops into his pocket. Voldemort, sensing Harry's lie, commands Quirrell to seize him. Harry's touch causes Quirrell immense pain, and in a desperate struggle, Harry grabs Quirrell's face, causing it to blister, before falling into unconsciousness.

scenes.summary: Harry awakens in the Hospital Wing, disoriented but alive. Dumbledore appears, revealing he arrived in time to save Harry and that the Sorcerer's Stone has been destroyed. He explains the Mirror of Erised's true purpose and the protective power of Harry's mother's love. Dumbledore also clarifies Snape's true role in protecting Harry, despite his hatred for James Potter. Ron and Hermione burst in, relieved, and Harry recounts his terrifying encounter. Hagrid then arrives, overcome with guilt for inadvertently revealing Fluffy's weakness, and gives Harry a photo album of his parents.

scenes.summary: Harry attends the end-of-year feast, initially seeing the Great Hall decked in Slytherin colors. Dumbledore, in his usual eccentric style, begins the House Cup ceremony, announcing Slytherin as the winner. However, he then awards last-minute points for bravery: fifty to Ron for chess, fifty to Hermione for logic, sixty to Harry for courage, and ten to Neville for standing up to his friends. This dramatic turn of events causes the Great Hall to transform into Gryffindor colors, securing their victory in the House Cup. Snape is forced to shake McGonagall's hand with a forced smile, confirming his continued animosity towards Harry.

threadMutations.threadDescription: The rivalry between Gryffindor and Slytherin houses.

scenes.summary: After passing their exams, Harry, Ron, and Hermione board the Hogwarts Express for the journey home. They reflect on the year, and Harry receives a warning about using magic over the holidays. At King's Cross, they pass through the barrier to the Muggle world, where Ginny Weasley points out Harry. Mrs. Weasley greets Harry warmly, but the Dursleys arrive, looking terrified at Harry's presence. Uncle Vernon rudely rushes Harry away, but Harry, with a grin, reveals to Ron and Hermione his plan to torment Dudley during the summer, knowing the Dursleys are unaware of the rule against magic outside of school.
